notes: Normal mode row
quantityText: Jack 'O Lantern Launcher + Explosive Jack 'O Lantern (25–50)

notes: Normal mode row
Jellyfish Blocks are Hardmode blocks purchased from the Pirate in a Graveyard for 50 CC each. Their main use is crafting Jellyfish furniture and Jellyfish Walls . They glow in the dark when placed.

conditions: In addition to being crafted, Jester's Arrows can be found in Gold Chests in the Cavern layer .

notes: The Jetpack can be purchased from the Steampunker for 40 GC during the following moon phases :

quantityText: Jim's Cap (Only if name is Jim)

notes: The Kwad Racer Drone is a Hardmode , post- Plantera novelty item purchased from the Cyborg for 10 GC . Pressing the ⚒ Use / Attack button while selected in the hotbar summons a harmless drone that can be controlled by the ▲ Up , ◀ Left , and ▶ Right buttons. Pressing ⚒ Use / Attack again or switching to another item instantly destroys the drone. The player appears to wield a remote control when the drone is in use.

The FPV Goggles are a Hardmode , post- Plantera accessory purchased from the Cyborg for 10 GC . It centers the screen on the Kwad Racer Drone or Wall Racer Car while one is active, allowing the player to fly the drone or drive the car without having to keep it within the screen, or to use it to scout out areas that are difficult to reach.

The Jousting Lance is a Hardmode melee weapon purchased from the Zoologist for 6 GC after filling out 30% (164 entries) of the Bestiary . When attacking using ⚒ Use / Attack , the lance will extend, and holding ⚒ Use / Attack will keep it extended at a fixed angle. The damage and knockback will increase depending on the player's movement speed , making mounts , sprinting accessories , or dash abilities (such as those granted by Shield of Cthulhu , Tabi / Master Ninja Gear , or Solar Flare armor ) useful for maximizing damage. When the player takes damage, the Jousting Lance will stop being active.

conditions: The Jungle Crate is a pre-Hardmode crate that can only be fished in the Jungle . It contains items found in standard crates and always contains an item normally found in Ivy Chests within Jungle Shrines or Living Mahogany Trees . Its Hardmode counterpart is the Bramble Crate .

The Bramble Crate is a Hardmode crate that can only be fished in the Jungle . Bramble Crates contain items found in standard crates, and they always contain an item normally found in Ivy Chests within Jungle Shrines . Its pre-Hardmode counterpart is the Jungle Crate .

Jungle Grass Seeds are seeds used to plant Jungle grass in Mud Blocks , which can potentially create an artificial Jungle or Underground Jungle (depending on depth) if enough Jungle grass and plants are created. Jungle Grass Seeds can be found by cutting down plants growing on Jungle grass using virtually any tool or weapon.

The Jungle Rose is a vanity accessory which can be harvested from the floor of the Jungle and Underground Jungle . Certain small red flowers growing on Jungle grass have a 1/20 (5%) chance to drop the item when cut. [ 1 ]

Jungle Spores are crafting materials acquired by destroying green glowing orbs that grow randomly on Jungle grass in the Underground Jungle , in or below the Cavern layer . Each orb drops 2–3 Jungle Spores. Harvested Jungle Spores cannot be replanted.

The Jungle Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in the Underground Jungle , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained by purchasing it from the Dryad for 10 CC each, in Hardmode . It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

notes: The Katana is a pre-Hardmode broadsword that can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 5 GC . It has a base 19% critical strike chance , as opposed to the usual 4%.

conditions: The Keg is a crafting station used to brew Ale . In addition to being crafted, it can sometimes be found in Underground Cabins .

notes: Normal mode row
Normal mode row
Normal mode row
The Kimono is a vanity item purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 1 GC .

The Land Mine is a unique Hardmode , post- Plantera placeable item. They remain dormant once placed, until a character touches them or they are activated by Wire , at which point they explode, dealing 250 damage. They are purchased from the Demolitionist for 5 GC each, after Plantera and at least one Pirate Invasion have been defeated.

The Mechanical Ruler is an informational accessory that, when equipped, draws a cyan, semi-transparent grid overlay on the screen, making individual block distances much clearer. This grid fades out when the player begins moving and returns once movement stops. The row and column of the block the mouse is hovering over are also highlighted in red, drawing a cross on the screen with the cursor at its center. The Mechanical Ruler can be purchased from the Mechanic for 1 GC .

conditions: The Lava Charm is an accessory that grants immunity to lava for 7 seconds. It is found in Chests located in the cavern layer as well as obtained from Hellstone Crates and Obsidian Crates with a 1/20 (5%) chance. It can only be found in Chests in the lower portion of the Caverns where lava pools can naturally spawn, as opposed to Chests throughout the entirety of the Caverns.

notes: The Lava Lamp is a furniture item purchased from the Party Girl for 2 GC , that emits a small amount of light and has an animated texture. It is not a mechanism but can be placed on tables . It is not considered a suitable light source for an NPC house . It is used purely as a decorative light source.
The Lawn Flamingo is a decorative furniture item that can be placed and serves no purpose other than as decoration. It can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 5 GC .

The Lawn Mower is a tool that can be purchased from the Golfer for 1 GC and mows normal and Hallowed grass . Mowed grass has a different texture, reduces enemy spawn chance by 10%, prevents grass plants (and dye plants) from growing, and allows Golf Balls to travel on top of it with unhindered movement while golfing . The Lawn Mower deals a fixed 8 classless damage to entities only while the player is moving forwards, is not in the air, and is not grappled. [ 1 ] When it is used on grass other than normal and hallowed, it only removes weeds and flowers .

conditions: Lead Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore . It spawns in the Underground and Cavern layers , and occasionally on the surface . Lead Ore also has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes .

notes: It cannot be obtained in a Crimson world unless the player makes an artificial Corruption biome (e.g. using Corrupt Seeds , purchased from the Dryad in a Graveyard (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) ) or uses a Corrupt Statue .

The Decay Chamber is a Hardmode crafting station used to craft Lesion furniture ( Corruption -themed furniture) out of Lesion Blocks . The Decay Chamber can be purchased from the Steampunker for 10 GC but only appears for sale in worlds that are generated with the Corruption . It can also be crafted from the Flesh Cloning Vat , its Crimson counterpart.

conditions: The Decay Chamber is a Hardmode crafting station used to craft Lesion furniture ( Corruption -themed furniture) out of Lesion Blocks . The Decay Chamber can be purchased from the Steampunker for 10 GC but only appears for sale in worlds that are generated with the Corruption . It can also be crafted from the Flesh Cloning Vat , its Crimson counterpart.

notes: The Lesser Mana Potion is a recovery potion that restores 50 mana when used and inflicts the Mana Sickness debuff upon use. It can be purchased from the Merchant for 1 SC each, and can also be found in Wooden and Pearlwood Crates obtained from fishing .
The Lever is a 2×2 activation mechanism purchased from the Mechanic for 30 SC . It can activate Statues , lights, etc. via ⚷ Open / Activate .
The Bunny License is a consumable item that can be purchased from the Zoologist for 5 GC after filling out 45% (246 entries) of the Bestiary .
The Cat License is a consumable item that can be purchased from the Zoologist for 5 GC .
The Dog License is a consumable item that can be purchased from the Zoologist for 5 GC after filling out 25% (137 entries) of the Bestiary .

conditions: Life Crystals can be found naturally placed in any area in the Underground layer and below, though they are fairly uncommon. They cannot be found in the Underworld or the Dungeon . Life Crystals also have a 1/8 (12.5%) chance to drop from a Golden Crate , or 19/160 (11.88%) chance from a Titanium Crate .

notes: The Lifeform Analyzer can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 5 GC .

conditions: The Key of Light is a craftable item which spawns a Hallowed Mimic when placed in any slot of any empty Chest . The world must be in Hardmode and the only item inside the Chest must be a single Key of Light.

The Lightning Bug is a harmless Hardmode critter that gives off a tiny amount of light, similarly to the Firefly . It spawns on Hallowed grass blocks during the night . The Lightning Bug has only 5 health and no defense , and can be damaged by other enemies and players.

notes: The Lightning Carrot is a pet-summoning item that spawns a Volt Bunny that follows the player. While flying to keep pace with the player, the Volt Bunny dashes and releases a series of spherical blue sparks, which emit a decent amount of light while moving erratically and can spread light over an area. If there are no available tiles for the pet to "sit" on, like when climbing walls or flying, the Volt Bunny will constantly jump around. The Lightning Carrot can be purchased from the Zoologist for 50 GC after completing 50% (273 entries) of the Bestiary .

conditions: The Lihzahrd Altar is a furniture item used for summoning Golem with Lihzahrd Power Cells . Upon pressing ⚷ Open / Activate on it, one Lihzahrd Power Cell is consumed from the player's inventory and Golem is instantly summoned. The Lihzahrd Altar is always found naturally placed in the final chamber of the Jungle Temple .

The Lihzahrd Brick Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in the Jungle Temple , considered unsafe . It drops as an item when destroyed, and can also be obtained via crafting. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

Lihzahrd Chests are chests found in the Jungle Temple . Each contains one Lihzahrd Power Cell , and may contain a Lihzahrd Furnace , some Solar Tablet Fragments , and sometimes, a Solar Tablet . They also contain other typical Gold Chest loot.

notes: The Lihzahrd Furnace is a Hardmode , post- Plantera crafting station used to craft Lihzahrd furniture out of Lihzahrd Bricks . It is always found in every Lihzahrd Chest in the Jungle Temple . Once Golem has been defeated, it can also be purchased from the Steampunker for 10 GC when she is spoken to in the Jungle .

conditions: The Lihzahrd Furnace is a Hardmode , post- Plantera crafting station used to craft Lihzahrd furniture out of Lihzahrd Bricks . It is always found in every Lihzahrd Chest in the Jungle Temple . Once Golem has been defeated, it can also be purchased from the Steampunker for 10 GC when she is spoken to in the Jungle .

One Lihzahrd Power Cell is guaranteed to be found within each Lihzahrd Chest , and they additionally have a 1/50 (2%) chance of dropping from Lihzahrds and Flying Snakes , both of which spawn exclusively within the Jungle Temple.

The Lime Kelp is a dye material and type of plant . It grows on any tiles submerged in at least 2 tiles of any liquid in the Underground and Cavern layers. [ 1 ] Unlike many plants, instead of broken by any attack, it has to be harvested with a pickaxe . Its sole purpose is to make Lime Dye .

The Lizard Egg is a pet-summoning item that spawns a Pet Lizard that follows the player. When the player is far enough away or in midair, the Pet Lizard will sprout wings like those of a Flying Snake to catch up with the player. The Lizard Egg has a 1/1000 (0.1%) chance of being dropped by Flying Snakes and Lihzahrds in the Jungle Temple .
The Loom is a crafting station used to craft Silk , as well as some items that require Silk as an ingredient. It can sometimes be found in Underground Cabins .

conditions: Luminite is a Hardmode ore dropped by the Moon Lord in stacks of 70–90 ( 90–110 ). It does not naturally generate in the world, and can be mined using any pickaxe .
